summary: This is a brief podcast announcement post for Stack Overflow Podcast #27, featuring Reddit founders Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian as guests. Joel also shares a technical warning about Microsoft SQL Server 2008's full text search being significantly slower than the 2005 version due to a new architecture. The post serves primarily as a pointer to the podcast episode and a cautionary note for developers considering a database upgrade.
key_points: Stack Overflow Podcast #27 featured Reddit co-founders Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian as guests
Microsoft SQL Server 2008 introduced a new full text search architecture that performs significantly worse than SQL Server 2005
Software upgrades don't always mean improvements — newer versions can regress in specific areas
money_quotes: Jeff recently upgraded the database server from Microsoft SQL Server 2005 to 2008, and found pretty conclusively that 2008 has a new architecture for full text search which is significantly slower than it was in 2005.
key_insight: Database version upgrades can introduce performance regressions, as demonstrated by SQL Server 2008's slower full text search compared to its predecessor.
